reason: The passage explicitly describes God's forthcoming destruction of Sisera's army and Jabin's kingdom. Deborah prophesies that 'the Lord has given Sisera into your hand' and that God will draw out to the Kishon River 'Sisera, the commander of Jabin's army, with his chariots and his multitude.' The narrative confirms this destruction: 'The Lord defeated Sisera and all his chariots and all his camp with the edge of the sword' and 'all of Sisera's camp fell by the sword, not one was left.' This is explicitly a supernatural destruction by Yahweh ('God subdued...Jabin, king of Canaan') that is collective and public in scale—affecting an entire military force (900 iron chariots) and their commander, not an individual.
